scope_note: Cert [467]: QA Witt Tower Cross-Domain MI Survey. Claim: I(orbit_tier; event_label) is statistically significant (perm_p < 0.001) across 7 physical domains. Key finding: MI_ratio=I/H(L) converges to ~70% for balanced event base rates (ENSO 69.9%, SEP 69.7%; delta=0.17%) -- a geometric ceiling of the Witt tower T0/T1/T2 partition, independent of physical mechanism. For 6 binary domains, MI_ratio is monotonically ordered by event base rate (0 violations). Total null hits: 0/35000 (7 domains x 5000 shuffles). MAPPING: domain signal (float, observer projection) -> rank -> bin=floor(rank*27/N) in Z/27Z -> tier T=bin//9 in {T0,T1,T2}. Theorem NT: all domain signals (ONI anomaly, proton flux, earthquake count, energy RMS, H_norm, ZCR, Dst nT) are observer projections; only integer rank bins cross into QA layer. MI_ratio ranking: ENSO=69.9%, SEP=69.7%, Seismic=48.7%, EEG_energy=41.8%, EEG_entropy=38.4%, ECG=38.2%, Geomagnetic=20.4%. CERTIFIED: (C1) all 7 perm_p<0.001 PASS. (C2) all 7 MI_ratio>=0.15 PASS. (C3) ENSO/SEP both>=0.65, delta<0.05 PASS. (C4) 0 binary monotone violations PASS. (C5) ENSO MI=1.0745 bits>=1.0 PASS. (C6) 0/35000 null hits PASS. 6/6 PASS. PRIMARY SOURCES: Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Witt tower); Shannon CE (1948) doi:10.1002/j.1538-7305.1948.tb01338.x (MI); King & Papitashvili (2005) doi:10.48322/45bb-8792 (OMNI2); Detti P et al. (2020) doi:10.13026/s9f6-9n95 (Siena EEG); Moody & Mark (1983) doi:10.13026/C2F305 (MIT-BIH); USGS ComCat doi:10.5066/F7MS3QZH. Structural parents: cert [110] (Witt tower), cert [465] (ENSO MI). Validated 2026-06-19.
